fragments.content: Chemotherapy refers to cytotoxic drugs that kill rapidly dividing cells by interfering with cell division. Cell cycle phases: G0 (resting), G1 (growth), S (DNA synthesis), G2 (preparation), M (mitosis). Chemotherapy classes by mechanism: alkylating agents (cyclophosphamide, cisplatin) cross-link DNA, cell cycle non-specific; antimetabolites (5-FU, methotrexate, gemcitabine) mimic nucleotides, S-phase specific; antitumor antibiotics (doxorubicin, bleomycin) intercalate DNA or generate free radicals; plant alkaloids (vincristine, paclitaxel) disrupt microtubules, M-phase specific; topoisomerase inhibitors (etoposide, irinotecan) prevent DNA unwinding. Combination chemotherapy uses drugs with different mechanisms and non-overlapping toxicities to maximize tumor kill while minimizing resistance. Dose intensity (mg/m²/week) correlates with outcomes in many cancers.

Chemotherapy administration follows evidence-based protocols. Dosing calculated by body surface area (BSA) or weight; renal/hepatic adjustment required for many agents. Cycles typically every 1-3 weeks allowing bone marrow recovery. Common regimens: FOLFOX (colon cancer), AC-T (breast cancer), CHOP (lymphoma), BEP (testicular cancer). Pre-medications prevent reactions: dexamethasone, ondansetron, aprepitant for nausea; diphenhydramine for hypersensitivity. Growth factor support (G-CSF) reduces neutropenia risk when febrile neutropenia risk >20%. Dose modifications: reduce 20-25% for grade 3-4 toxicity; delay until counts recover (ANC >1500, platelets >100,000). Toxicity management: antiemetics per emetogenic risk; mucosal care for stomatitis; loperamide for diarrhea; erythropoietin for anemia (controversial). Tumor lysis syndrome prophylaxis with allopurinol/rasburicase for high-burden hematologic malignancies.

Chemotherapy toxicity monitored using CTCAE (Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events) grading 1-5. Hematologic: neutropenia (ANC <1500), febrile neutropenia (fever + ANC <500), thrombocytopenia (platelets <100,000), anemia (Hgb <10). Non-hematologic: neuropathy (sensory/motor), cardiotoxicity (anthracyclines - monitor LVEF), nephrotoxicity (cisplatin - monitor creatinine), hepatotoxicity (elevated transaminases), pulmonary toxicity (bleomycin), ototoxicity (cisplatin). Baseline assessment before each cycle: CBC with differential, comprehensive metabolic panel, performance status. Response assessment: RECIST criteria for solid tumors (complete response, partial response ≥30% decrease, stable disease, progressive disease ≥20% increase); PET response criteria for lymphoma. Long-term monitoring: secondary malignancies (AML/MDS after alkylators), infertility, cardiac dysfunction, cognitive changes ('chemo brain').
